collections.text: Import topology repository as undeployed lab

data.name: Import topology repository as undeployed lab

requests.name: Log in
requests.notes:
Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token.

Success: 200 JWT token

requests.name: Get system metrics
requests.notes:
Returns detailed CPU, memory, and disk metrics for the API server. Requires authentication.

Success: 200 System metrics

requests.name: Get API server health
requests.notes:
Returns basic health status for the API server.

Success: 200 Basic health information

requests.name: Stream containerlab events
requests.notes:
Streams containerlab events in real time as NDJSON (one JSON object per line).

**Notes**
- The response stays open until the client disconnects.

**Examples**
NDJSON (one JSON object per line):
```json
{"time":1706918400,"type":"container","action":"start","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl1","lab":"mylab","clab-node-name":"srl1","clab-node-kind":"nokia_srlinux"}}
{"time":1706918405,"type":"container","action":"start","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl2","lab":"mylab","clab-node-name":"srl2","clab-node-kind":"nokia_srlinux"}}
```


Interface stats (interfaceStats=true):
```json
{"time":1706918410,"type":"interface-stats","action":"stats","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl1","lab":"mylab","interface":"e1-1","rx_bytes":123456,"tx_bytes":654321}}
```

Success: 200 Event stream - NDJSON (one JSON object per line)

requests.name: Get containerlab version
requests.notes:
Returns version information for the containerlab library in use.

Success: 200 Containerlab version details

requests.name: Check containerlab updates
requests.notes:
Checks whether a newer containerlab release is available.

Success: 200 Result of the version check

requests.name: List users
requests.notes:
Returns a list of system users. Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 List of user details

requests.name: Create user
requests.notes:
Creates a new system user. Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 201 User created successfully

requests.name: Get user details
requests.notes:
Returns details for a specific user. Requires superuser privileges or the user's own account.

Success: 200 User details

requests.name: Update user
requests.notes:
Updates an existing user. Regular users may update only their own profile fields. Group and superuser status changes require superuser privileges.

Success: 200 User updated successfully

requests.name: Delete user
requests.notes:
Deletes a user from the system. Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 User deleted successfully

requests.name: Change user password
requests.notes:
Changes a user's password. Requires superuser privileges or the user's own account.

Success: 200 Password changed successfully

requests.name: List runtime images
requests.notes: Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Remove a runtime image
requests.notes: Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Pull a runtime image
requests.notes: Success: 200 OK

requests.name: List lab custom icons
requests.notes:
Returns the custom TopoViewer icons available for a lab, merging lab-local .clab-icons with the global ~/.clab/icons library. Lab-local icons take precedence.

Success: 200 Merged lab icon list

requests.name: Reconcile lab custom icons
requests.notes:
Copies used custom icons from the global ~/.clab/icons library into the lab-local .clab-icons directory and removes unused lab-local custom icons.

Success: 200 Reconcile success

requests.name: List custom node templates
requests.notes:
Returns the authenticated user's persisted TopoViewer custom node templates. If none have been saved yet, seeded defaults matching VS Code are returned.

Success: 200 Custom node templates

requests.name: Save custom node template
requests.notes:
Creates or updates a single TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.

Success: 200 Updated custom node templates

requests.name: Replace custom node templates
requests.notes:
Replaces the authenticated user's full TopoViewer custom node template collection.

Success: 200 Updated custom node templates

requests.name: Set default custom node template
requests.notes:
Sets the default TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.

Success: 200 Updated custom node templates

requests.name: Delete custom node template
requests.notes:
Deletes a single TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.

Success: 200 Updated custom node templates

requests.name: List global custom icons
requests.notes:
Returns the authenticated user's global custom TopoViewer icon library from ~/.clab/icons.

Success: 200 Global custom icons

requests.name: Upload global custom icon
requests.notes:
Uploads a new global custom TopoViewer icon into ~/.clab/icons for the authenticated user.

Success: 200 Upload success

requests.name: Delete global custom icon
requests.notes:
Deletes a global custom TopoViewer icon from ~/.clab/icons for the authenticated user.

Success: 200 Delete success

requests.name: List labs
requests.notes:
Returns details for all running labs.

**Notes**
- Results are filtered by owner unless the caller is a superuser.

Success: 200 All labs

requests.name: Deploy lab
requests.notes:
Deploys a containerlab topology.

**Notes**
- The request body must include either `topologyContent` or `topologySourceUrl` (not both).

Success: 200 Deployed lab details

requests.name: Deploy lab from archive
requests.notes:
Deploys a containerlab topology provided as a .zip or .tar.gz archive.

Success: 200 Deployed lab details

requests.name: List editable lab topology files
requests.notes:
Returns editable topology entries from the authenticated user's lab directory.

Success: 200 Topology entries

requests.name: Import topology repository as undeployed lab
requests.notes:
Clones a supported Git repository URL and registers it as an undeployed lab.

Success: 200 Import result

requests.name: Inspect lab
requests.notes:
Returns details for a specific running lab.

Success: 200 Lab containers

requests.name: Redeploy lab
requests.notes:
Redeploys a lab by name.

**Notes**
- This operation destroys the lab and then deploys it again.
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 Redeployed lab details

requests.name: Destroy lab
requests.notes:
Destroys a lab by name after verifying ownership.

**Notes**
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 Lab destroyed successfully

requests.name: Deploy on-disk topology for lab
requests.notes:
Deploys an on-disk topology from the authenticated user's lab directory.

**Notes**
- `path` defaults to `<labName>.clab.yml` when omitted.
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 Deployed lab details

requests.name: Execute command in lab
requests.notes:
Executes a command on nodes within a lab.

Success: 200 Execution result

requests.name: List lab interfaces
requests.notes:
Returns interface details for nodes in a lab.

Success: 200 Interface details

requests.name: Restart lab nodes
requests.notes:
Restarts all nodes in a deployed lab while preserving containerlab dataplane links.

**Notes**
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Save lab configuration
requests.notes:
Saves the running configuration for nodes in a lab.

Success: 200 Configuration saved

requests.name: Start lab nodes
requests.notes:
Starts all nodes in a deployed lab while preserving containerlab dataplane links.

**Notes**
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Stop lab nodes
requests.notes:
Stops all nodes in a deployed lab while preserving containerlab dataplane links.

**Notes**
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Get lab annotations
requests.notes:
Returns annotations for the specified lab. For deployed labs, the running annotations path is preferred and local files are used as fallback.

Success: 200 Annotations content

requests.name: Update lab annotations
requests.notes:
Updates annotations for the specified lab. For deployed labs, writes to the running annotations path when present and otherwise writes to local files.

Success: 200 Write success

requests.name: Stream topology document events
requests.notes:
Streams topology YAML/annotations change events for a single lab topology document pair as NDJSON.

Success: 200 Topology document event stream

requests.name: Read lab topology file
requests.notes:
Reads a file from within the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

Success: 200 File content

requests.name: Write lab topology file
requests.notes:
Writes a file inside the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

Success: 200 Write success

requests.name: Delete lab topology file
requests.notes:
Deletes a file inside the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

Success: 200 Delete success

requests.name: Check lab topology file existence
requests.notes:
Checks whether a file exists inside the specified lab directory.

Success: 200 File exists

requests.name: Rename lab topology file
requests.notes:
Renames or moves a file inside the specified lab directory using scoped relative paths.

Success: 200 Rename success

requests.name: Get lab topology YAML
requests.notes:
Returns the topology YAML for the specified lab. For deployed labs, the running topology source path is preferred and local files are used as fallback.

Success: 200 Topology YAML content

requests.name: Update lab topology YAML

raw.data:
name: srl01
topology:
  nodes:
    srl1:
      kind: nokia_srlinux

requests.notes:
Updates the topology YAML for the specified lab. For deployed labs, writes to the running topology source when present and otherwise writes to local files.

Success: 200 Write success

requests.name: Get node browser ports
requests.notes:
Returns exposed host ports for a node suitable for opening in a browser.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Pause node
requests.notes:
Pauses a running node in a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Restart node
requests.notes:
Restarts a node in a lab while preserving containerlab dataplane links.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Start node
requests.notes:
Starts a stopped node in a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Stop node
requests.notes:
Stops a running node in a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Unpause node
requests.notes:
Unpauses a paused node in a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Get node logs
requests.notes:
Returns logs for a lab node.

**Notes**
- When `follow=true`, the response streams as NDJSON (one JSON object per line) until the client disconnects or the 30-minute timeout.

Success: 200 Container logs (follow=false). When follow=true, response is NDJSON stream of LogLine objects.

requests.name: Request temporary SSH access details
requests.notes:
Creates temporary SSH access to a lab node and returns connection details for an external SSH client.
This endpoint does not create a browser terminal session. For browser terminals, use `/api/v1/labs/{labName}/nodes/{nodeName}/terminal-sessions` with `protocol=ssh`.

Success: 200 SSH connection details

requests.name: List SSH sessions
requests.notes:
Returns active SSH sessions.

**Notes**
- Regular users see only their sessions.
- Superusers can include all sessions via the `all` query parameter.

Success: 200 List of active SSH sessions

requests.name: Terminate SSH session
requests.notes:
Terminates a specific SSH session by port.

Success: 200 Session terminated successfully

requests.name: Create browser terminal session
requests.notes:
Creates a constrained interactive terminal session for an owned lab node using one of the supported protocols: `ssh`, `shell`, or `telnet`.

**Protocol behavior**
- `ssh`: launches the server-side `ssh` client in a PTY directly to the node management IP and streams that terminal over WebSocket.
- `shell`: launches a PTY-backed `docker|podman exec -it <container> <server-selected-command>` session.
- `telnet`: launches a PTY-backed `docker|podman exec -it <container> telnet 127.0.0.1 <port>` session.

**Security notes**
- The backend chooses the launch command for every protocol. Clients choose only the protocol and terminal size.
- Access is limited to owned nodes unless the caller is a configured superuser.

**Relationship to `/api/v1/labs/{labName}/nodes/{nodeName}/ssh`**
- This endpoint creates the interactive browser terminal session.
- The separate `/ssh` endpoint returns temporary SSH access details for an external SSH client and is not required for browser terminals.

Success: 200 Created terminal session

requests.name: Get terminal session
requests.notes:
Returns metadata and lifecycle state for a terminal session.

Success: 200 Terminal session metadata

requests.name: Terminate terminal session
requests.notes:
Terminates a terminal session owned by the caller (or any session for a superuser).

Success: 200 Terminal session terminated

requests.name: Stream terminal session
requests.notes:
Upgrades the request to a WebSocket connection for terminal input, output, resize, and close events.

**Protocol notes**
- Connect with a WebSocket client to this endpoint after creating a terminal session.
- Client messages are JSON objects with `type` set to `input`, `resize`, or `close`.
- Server messages are JSON objects with `type` set to `ready`, `output`, or `exit`.

requests.name: GoTTY share action
requests.notes:
Executes GoTTY share action (attach, detach, reattach) for a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: SSHX share action
requests.notes:
Executes SSHX share action (attach, detach, reattach) for a lab.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Run fcli command
requests.notes:
Runs an fcli command against the selected lab topology.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Generate draw.io graph
requests.notes:
Generates a draw.io diagram from a lab topology and returns the generated XML content.

Success: 200 OK

requests.name: Delete all Wireshark VNC sessions
requests.notes:
Terminates all capture VNC sessions owned by the authenticated user. Superusers terminate all sessions.

Success: 200 Capture sessions terminated

requests.name: Delete Wireshark VNC session
requests.notes:
Terminates a capture VNC session and stops the corresponding Wireshark container.

Success: 200 Capture session terminated

requests.name: Get Wireshark VNC session readiness
requests.notes:
Returns whether a capture VNC session is ready and its proxied URL path.

Success: 200 Readiness state

requests.name: Proxy Wireshark VNC assets
requests.notes:
Proxies noVNC HTTP assets for a capture session. WebSocket upgrades on this path are used by noVNC but are not represented in Swagger.

Success: 200 Proxied noVNC asset response

requests.name: Build packetflix capture URI
requests.notes:
Builds packetflix URI(s) for the provided lab interface targets (non-VNC capture mode).

Success: 200 Packetflix capture URI payload

requests.name: Create Wireshark VNC capture session(s)
requests.notes:
Creates one or more Wireshark noVNC capture sessions for the specified lab interface targets.

Success: 200 Created capture sessions

requests.name: Install EdgeShark
requests.notes:
Installs and starts EdgeShark services on the host runtime using compose.

Success: 200 EdgeShark installed

requests.name: Get EdgeShark status
requests.notes:
Returns whether EdgeShark is reachable and its runtime details.

Success: 200 EdgeShark status

requests.name: Uninstall EdgeShark
requests.notes:
Stops and removes EdgeShark services managed by compose.

Success: 200 EdgeShark uninstalled

requests.name: Generate containerlab topology
requests.notes:
Generates a containerlab topology from CLOS definitions and can optionally deploy it for the authenticated user.

**Notes**
- Deployment is denied if a lab with the target name already exists.
- The `images` and `licenses` fields map node kind to image or license path (e.g., {"nokia_srlinux":"ghcr.io/..."}).
- When `deploy=true`, the topology is saved to the user's `~/.clab/<labName>/` directory and `outputFile` is ignored.
- When `deploy=false` and `outputFile` is empty, YAML is returned in the response.
- When `deploy=false` and `outputFile` is set, the file is saved to that path on the server (requires API server write permissions).

Success: 200 Generation successful (YAML or deploy output)

requests.name: Disable TX checksum offload
requests.notes:
Disables TX checksum offload on the eth0 interface of a container. Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 Offload disabled successfully

requests.name: Create certificate authority (CA)
requests.notes:
Creates a CA certificate and private key. Requires superuser privileges.

**Notes**
- Files are stored in the user's `~/.clab/certs/<ca_name>/` directory on the server.

Success: 200 CA created successfully

requests.name: Sign certificate
requests.notes:
Creates a certificate/key and signs it with a previously generated CA. Requires superuser privileges.

**Notes**
- Files are stored in the user's `~/.clab/certs/<ca_name>/` directory.

Success: 200 Certificate signed successfully

requests.name: Create vEth pair
requests.notes:
Creates a virtual Ethernet (vEth) pair between two endpoints (container, host, bridge, or ovs-bridge). Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 vEth pair created successfully

requests.name: Create VxLAN tunnel
requests.notes:
Creates a VxLAN tunnel interface and sets up tc rules for traffic redirection. Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 VxLAN tunnel created successfully

requests.name: Delete VxLAN tunnels by prefix
requests.notes:
Deletes VxLAN tunnel interfaces that match the provided prefix (default: `vx-`). Requires superuser privileges.

Success: 200 VxLAN tunnels deleted successfully

requests.name: Reset link impairments (netem)
requests.notes:
Resets (removes) netem impairments from a specific interface of a containerlab node owned by the authenticated user. Superusers may operate on any node.

Success: 200 Impairments reset successfully

requests.name: Set link impairments (netem)
requests.notes:
Sets netem impairments (delay, jitter, loss, rate limiting, corruption) on a specific interface of a containerlab node owned by the authenticated user. Superusers may operate on any node.

Success: 200 Impairments set successfully

requests.name: Show link impairments (netem)
requests.notes:
Lists netem impairments for a containerlab node owned by the authenticated user. Superusers may inspect any node.

Success: 200 Netem impairments
